{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/253079834?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/OoYo0uto", "id": 253079834, "login": "OoYo0uto", "node_id": "U_kgDODxWxGg", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/OoYo0uto", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-52817", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.0, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:L/AC:L/AT:N/PR:H/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N/E:P"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-88", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')"}], "description": "### Summary
In the [Debian.sudoers](https://github.com/Linuxfabrik/monitoring-plugins/blob/main/assets/sudoers/Debian.sudoers) file, `apt-get` is allowed for the nagios user. The full command including the arguments are not enforced and can therefore be choosen arbitrarily. This allows to easily get a root shell as the nagios user:

### PoC
By choosing a particular argument, you can get (as a nagios user) a root shell:
```
sudo apt-get update -o APT::Update::Pre-Invoke::="/bin/sh"
```
Since the nagious user can use sudo to run apt-get as root, the resulting shell is also running as root.

### Impact
The vulnerability is a local privilege escalation, impacting users who use the provided sudoers file. It requires that an attacker already compromised the nagios account (which is quite a high barrier to be honest).

### Fix
Since only one place where `apt-get` is currently used (in [deb-updates](https://github.com/Linuxfabrik/monitoring-plugins/blob/998302a5fb43e89df1359f4cbb6558f81c96ae4f/check-plugins/deb-updates/deb-updates#L124)) was found, it should be enough to allow only the specific arguments used there.

Here an example how the line in the sudoers file could look like:
```
                    /usr/lib64/nagios/plugins/strongswan-connections,\
                    /usr/lib64/nagios/plugins/systemd-unit,\
                    /usr/bin/apt-get update --quiet 2
```", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-02T20:23:56Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-52817"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-02T20:23:56Z", "references": ["https://github.com/Linuxfabrik/monitoring-plugins/security/advisories/GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/Linuxfabrik/monitoring-plugins/security-advisories/GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/Linuxfabrik/monitoring-plugins", "summary": "Linuxfabrik Monitoring Plugins: Sudoers may be able to obtain privilege escalation via /usr/bin/apt-get arguments", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-02T20:23:57Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-8w6w-23mq-h8rg", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "5.1.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "linuxfabrik-lib"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 5.0.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Linuxfabrik Monitoring Plugins: Sudoers may be able to obtain privilege escalation via /usr/bin/apt-get arguments
